title: Strobilanthes dalhousieana
taxonomy: Strobilanthes dalhousieana is classified under the family Acanthaceae. This classification places it within the order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants. Within the family Acanthaceae, Strobilanthes dalhousieana is also referred to as a member of the genus Strobilanthes. intro: Strobilanthes dalhousieana, also known as the blue mountain agastache, is a flowering plant native to the Eastern Himalayas, characterized by its blue-purple flowers and aromatic foliage. The health benefits of Strobilanthes dalhousieana include its potential use as an anti-inflammatory agent, an antimicrobial agent, and an antioxidant, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Strobilanthes dalhousieana include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
